Brand Pattern Library

Visual reference and copy-pasteable code for every Fluid brand building block.
For agents: load the code snippet, adapt it, ship it.

Color Palette

Two distinct palettes: Social and Website. Never cross-pollinate.

Accent Colors Weight: 95

One accent color per design. Used across all contexts — social, website, presentations.

#FF8B58
Orange — Urgency, pain, warning, CTAs
#42b1ff
Blue — Technical, intelligence, trust, links
#44b574
Green — Success, solution, proof
#c985e5
Purple — Premium, financial, analytical
Copy: Accent color CSS
CSS 
/* Brand Accent Colors — pick ONE per design */
.accent-orange  { color: #FF8B58; }  /* Urgency, pain, warning */
.accent-blue    { color: #42b1ff; }  /* Technical, intelligence, trust */
.accent-green   { color: #44b574; }  /* Success, solution, proof */
.accent-purple  { color: #c985e5; }  /* Premium, financial, analytical */

/* CSS variable form */
:root {
  --clr-orange: #FF8B58;
  --clr-blue: #42b1ff;
  --clr-green: #44b574;
  --clr-purple: #c985e5;
}

Neutrals Weight: 90

#000000
Pure black — social backgrounds
#050505
Near-black — website backgrounds
#111 / #161616
Section backgrounds
#ffffff
Primary text (white)
#f5f0e8
Body text — warm off-white
#888888
Secondary text

RGBA Overlay Patterns

rgba(255,255,255,0.45) — Body copy 85
rgba(255,255,255,0.25) — Dimmed/secondary 80
rgba(255,255,255,0.03) — Card backgrounds 80
rgba(255,255,255,0.06) — Card borders 75
#1a1a1a / #222 — Borders/dividers 75
Copy: Neutrals CSS
CSS 
/* Neutrals — backgrounds, text, overlays */
:root {
  --clr-bg: #000;             /* Social: pure black */
  --clr-bg-page: #050505;     /* Website: near-black */
  --clr-bg-section: #111;     /* Section fills */
  --clr-text: #ffffff;        /* Primary text */
  --clr-text-warm: #f5f0e8;   /* Body text (warm) */
  --clr-text-secondary: #888; /* Supporting text */
  --clr-border: #1a1a1a;      /* Borders, dividers */
}
.body-copy    { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.45); }  /* Weight: 85 */
.dimmed       { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.25); }  /* Weight: 80 */
.card         { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.03); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06); }

Typography

FLFont + NeueHaas + Inter across all deliverables.

Font Mapping

Display Name CSS font-family File Context Weight
FLFont Bold flfontbold assets/fonts/flfontbold.ttf Social — taglines 95
NeueHaasDisplay NeueHaas assets/fonts/Inter-VariableFont.ttf (proxy) Social — headlines, body 90
Inter Inter assets/fonts/Inter-VariableFont.ttf Body, UI text 80

FLFont Bold Samples

Every transaction matters.

FLFont Bold / 32px / accent orange — Instagram tagline

One connection. Zero 3am calls.

FLFont Bold / 26px / accent blue — Instagram tagline (smaller)

Every transaction gets its best shot.

FLFont Bold / 22px / accent green — LinkedIn tagline

Copy: FLFont @font-face and usage
CSS 
@font-face {
  font-family: 'flfontbold';
  src: url('assets/fonts/flfontbold.ttf') format('truetype');
  font-weight: 700;
  font-style: normal;
}

/* Usage — taglines and emphasis ONLY */
.flfont-tagline {
  font-family: 'flfontbold', cursive;
  font-size: 26px;       /* Instagram: 26-32px, LinkedIn: 20-24px */
  color: #FF8B58;         /* Replace with post accent color */
  line-height: 1.3;
}

NeueHaasDisplay / Inter Samples (Social)

When a transaction fails

NeueHaasDisplay Black 900 / 82px / uppercase / letter-spacing: -0.03em — Instagram headline

Half your transactions

NeueHaasDisplay Black 900 / 52px / uppercase / letter-spacing: -0.03em — LinkedIn headline

The order went through. It never reached the commission engine. The rep who closed the deal? They'll never know.

NeueHaasDisplay Light 300 / 22px / Body copy / rgba(255,255,255,0.45)

Copy: NeueHaas @font-face and typography scale
CSS 
@font-face {
  font-family: 'NeueHaas';
  src: url('assets/fonts/Inter-VariableFont.ttf') format('truetype');
  font-weight: 100 900;
  font-style: normal;
}

/* Social Typography Scale — Instagram */
.headline-ig {
  font-family: 'NeueHaas', sans-serif;
  font-weight: 900;
  font-size: 82px;       /* Range: 82-100px */
  line-height: 0.92;
  letter-spacing: -0.03em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: #ffffff;
}
.headline-accent {
  color: #FF8B58;         /* Replace with post accent color */
}
.body-copy {
  font-family: 'NeueHaas', sans-serif;
  font-weight: 300;
  font-size: 22px;       /* Range: 22-24px */
  line-height: 1.5;
  color: rgba(255,255,255,0.45);
}
.side-label {
  font-family: 'NeueHaas', sans-serif;
  font-size: 11px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.15em;
  opacity: 0.35;
  writing-mode: vertical-rl;
}
.slide-number {
  font-family: 'NeueHaas', sans-serif;
  font-size: 13px;
  opacity: 0.40;
}

/* Social Typography Scale — LinkedIn */
.headline-li {
  font-family: 'NeueHaas', sans-serif;
  font-weight: 900;
  font-size: 52px;       /* Range: 52-62px */
  line-height: 0.92;
  letter-spacing: -0.03em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: #ffffff;
}
.body-copy-li {
  font-family: 'NeueHaas', sans-serif;
  font-weight: 300;
  font-size: 16px;       /* Range: 16-18px */
  color: rgba(255,255,255,0.45);
}

Uppercase Patterns Weight: 90

All-caps is used selectively. The letter-spacing inverts based on size: large display text gets tight negative tracking, small functional text gets wide positive tracking.

ElementLetter-SpacingSizeNotes
Headlines -0.03em (tight) 82-100px+ Massive, fills the frame. Tight tracking creates density and impact.
Headline accents -0.03em (tight) Same as headline Accent-colored words within headlines. Same treatment.
Side labels 0.15em (wide) 11px Vertical rotated text on right edge. ~35% opacity.
Pills / tags 0.08em (wide) 10px Small tag-like elements with card background + border.
Context labels 0.1em (wide) 11-18px Stat descriptions, cost labels, status indicators. ~30% opacity.

NOT Uppercase

Body copy (sentence case) FLFont taglines (sentence case) Sub-text callouts (sentence case)

Live Examples

Your checkout is losing money

Headline — uppercase / -0.03em tracking / Black 900

Real-time Bidirectional SOC 2 Type II

Pills — uppercase / 0.08em tracking / 10px / accent color

Industry avg acceptance rate

Context label — uppercase / 0.1em tracking / ~30% opacity

Copy: Uppercase pattern CSS
CSS 
/* Headlines — tight tracking, massive size */
.headline {
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: -0.03em;
  font-weight: 900;
  line-height: 0.92;
}

/* Pills / tags — wide tracking, tiny size */
.pill {
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  font-size: 10px;
  font-weight: 700;
}

/* Context labels — wide tracking, dimmed */
.context-label {
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.1em;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: rgba(255,255,255,0.3);
}

/* Side labels — widest tracking, vertical */
.side-label {
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.15em;
  font-size: 11px;
  opacity: 0.35;
  writing-mode: vertical-rl;
}

Brushstroke Textures

11 brand brush textures. Use mix-blend-mode: screen and opacity 0.10-0.25. Always bleed off canvas edges.

brush-texture-01

Weight: 75

brush-texture-02

Weight: 75

brush-texture-03

Weight: 75

brush-texture-04

Weight: 75

brush-texture-05

Weight: 75

brush-texture-06

Weight: 75

brush-texture-07

Weight: 75

brush-texture-08

Weight: 75

brush-texture-09

Weight: 75

brush-texture-10

Weight: 75

brush-white

Large white brush stroke for bold overlays.

Weight: 75
Copy: Brushstroke CSS usage pattern
CSS + HTML 
/* Brushstroke Usage Rules:
   - ALWAYS use mix-blend-mode: screen (Weight: 95)
   - Opacity: 0.10-0.25 (Weight: 90)
   - Edges must bleed off canvas, never cut mid-frame (Weight: 85)
   - 2 per post standard, 3 max for manifesto (Weight: 80)
   - Slight rotation for organic feel (Weight: 50)
*/

/* Brushstroke positioning pattern */
.brushstroke {
  position: absolute;
  mix-blend-mode: screen;
  opacity: 0.15;          /* 0.10-0.15 subtle, 0.20-0.25 dramatic */
  pointer-events: none;
  z-index: 1;
}

/* Top-right placement (bleed off edge) */
.brushstroke-top-right {
  top: -20px;
  right: -30px;
  width: 500px;
  transform: rotate(5deg);
}

/* Bottom-left placement (bleed off edge) */
.brushstroke-bottom-left {
  bottom: -20px;
  left: -30px;
  width: 500px;
  transform: rotate(-8deg);
}

/* Full-height edge framing */
.brushstroke-edge-left {
  top: 0;
  left: -10px;
  height: 100%;
  width: auto;
}

/* Center-bottom grounding */
.brushstroke-ground {
  bottom: -10px;
  left: 50%;
  transform: translateX(-50%);
  width: 90%;
}

/* HTML usage */
<div class="post" style="position: relative; overflow: hidden;">
  <img class="brushstroke brushstroke-top-right"
       src="assets/brushstrokes/brush-texture-01.png"
       alt="" aria-hidden="true">
  <img class="brushstroke brushstroke-bottom-left"
       src="assets/brushstrokes/brush-texture-03.png"
       alt="" aria-hidden="true">

  <!-- Content here -->
</div>

Placement Strategies

StrategyBest BrushstrokesWhen
Top-right + bottom-leftdiagonal-sweep, compact-burstDefault for most posts
Full-height edge framingvertical-double-block, vertical-edge-sweepDramatic, editorial posts
Full-width top sweephorizontal-wideHorizon/sky effect
Both sides (curtain)Any tall verticalsManifesto posts only (sparingly)
Center-bottom groundingbottom-groundingUnder content, stability

Circles & Underlines

Hand-drawn emphasis elements for words and data points. CSS-mask technique — any color. Weight: 90

EMPHASIS ONLY, NOT DECORATIVE — Every circle/underline must wrap a specific word or data point. White PNG masks + backgroundColor = any color. Never use hue-rotate or tinted PNGs. 90
How it works — White PNG used as CSS mask-image. A colored div behind the text has its shape cut by the mask. Change backgroundColor to change the color. Works in any context: static HTML, social post images, presentations, web pages.
STATIC BY DEFAULT — For social post images, email graphics, one-pagers, and any static/image output: just use the mask technique with no animation. Animation is optional and only for interactive contexts (presentations, web pages, app UIs). Most brand assets are rendered as static images. 75

Circle Variants (6 masks)

assets/circles/circle-{1-6}.png — white masks, use as mask-image

circle-1

circle-2

circle-3

circle-4

circle-5

circle-6 (original sketch)

Underline Variants (3 masks)

assets/underlines/underline-{1-3}.png — white masks, use as mask-image

underline-1

underline-2

underline-3

Static Usage (default for images & social posts)

No animation needed. Just the mask + color. This is what you use for social post images, email headers, one-pagers, and any rendered-to-image output.

Try a color:
80% is considered healthy.
Hope for the best.

Animated Reveal (optional — presentations & web only)

For interactive contexts like slidev presentations, web pages, and app UIs. Circle uses a conic-gradient sweep from 12 o'clock; underline sweeps left-to-right. Both have noisy fade edges for an organic feel. Click to replay.

80% is considered healthy.

click to replay circle animation

Hope for the best.

click to replay underline animation

Copy: Circle emphasis (CSS mask technique)
CSS + HTML 
/* Circle Emphasis — CSS Mask Technique (Weight: 90)
   White PNG mask shapes the colored div behind text.
   Change backgroundColor for any color. No hue-rotate needed.
   Assets: assets/circles/circle-{1-6}.png
*/

.circled-wrapper {
  position: relative;
  display: inline-block;
  isolation: isolate;      /* creates stacking context */
}

.circled-text {
  position: relative;
  z-index: 1;
}

.circled-decoration {
  position: absolute;
  z-index: 0;
  pointer-events: none;
  left: -25%;
  top: -35%;
  width: 150%;
  height: 170%;
}

.circled-decoration-inner {
  display: block;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;

  /* THE KEY: color via background, shape via mask */
  background-color: #FF8B58;             /* <-- any color */
  -webkit-mask-image: url('assets/circles/circle-1.png');
  mask-image: url('assets/circles/circle-1.png');
  -webkit-mask-size: contain;
  mask-size: contain;
  -webkit-mask-repeat: no-repeat;
  mask-repeat: no-repeat;
  -webkit-mask-position: center;
  mask-position: center;
}

/* HTML */
<span class="circled-wrapper">
  <span class="circled-text">80%</span>
  <span class="circled-decoration">
    <span class="circled-decoration-inner"></span>
  </span>
</span>
Copy: Underline emphasis (CSS mask technique)
CSS + HTML 
/* Underline Emphasis — CSS Mask Technique (Weight: 90)
   Same mask approach as circles. Sits below the text baseline.
   thickness and offset are configurable.
   Assets: assets/underlines/underline-{1-3}.png
*/

.underlined-wrapper {
  position: relative;
  display: inline-block;
  isolation: isolate;      /* creates stacking context */
}

.underlined-text {
  position: relative;
  z-index: 1;
}

.underlined-decoration {
  position: absolute;
  z-index: 0;
  pointer-events: none;
  left: 0;
  right: 0;
  bottom: -0.1em;    /* offset — adjust per font */
  height: 0.55em;    /* thickness */
}

.underlined-decoration-inner {
  display: block;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;

  background-color: #FF8B58;             /* <-- any color */
  -webkit-mask-image: url('assets/underlines/underline-1.png');
  mask-image: url('assets/underlines/underline-1.png');
  -webkit-mask-size: 100% 100%;
  mask-size: 100% 100%;
  -webkit-mask-repeat: no-repeat;
  mask-repeat: no-repeat;
  -webkit-mask-position: center;
  mask-position: center;
}

/* HTML */
<span class="underlined-wrapper">
  <span class="underlined-text">best.</span>
  <span class="underlined-decoration">
    <span class="underlined-decoration-inner"></span>
  </span>
</span>
Copy: Animated reveal (conic sweep for circles, linear sweep for underlines)
JavaScript 
/* Animated Emphasis Reveal
   Circle: conic-gradient sweep from 12 o'clock with noisy fade edge
   Underline: left-to-right linear-gradient with noisy fade edge
   Both use requestAnimationFrame + easeInOutCubic
*/
const FADE_ZONE = 0.15, NOISE_STRENGTH = 0.7, NOISE_STOPS = 40;

function easeInOutCubic(t) {
  return t < 0.5 ? 4*t*t*t : 1 - Math.pow(-2*t + 2, 3) / 2;
}

// Circle: conic sweep from top
function circleRevealGradient(progress) {
  const sweepDeg = progress * 360, fadeDeg = FADE_ZONE * 360;
  const solidEnd = Math.max(0, sweepDeg - fadeDeg);
  const stops = [`rgba(0,0,0,1) 0deg`, `rgba(0,0,0,1) ${solidEnd.toFixed(1)}deg`];
  for (let i = 0; i <= NOISE_STOPS; i++) {
    const t = i / NOISE_STOPS, deg = solidEnd + t * fadeDeg;
    if (deg > sweepDeg) break;
    const baseAlpha = 1 - t;
    const noise = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 2 * NOISE_STRENGTH;
    const alpha = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, baseAlpha + noise * (1 - Math.abs(baseAlpha - 0.5) * 2)));
    stops.push(`rgba(0,0,0,${alpha.toFixed(3)}) ${deg.toFixed(1)}deg`);
  }
  stops.push(`rgba(0,0,0,0) ${sweepDeg.toFixed(1)}deg`, `rgba(0,0,0,0) 360deg`);
  return `conic-gradient(from -90deg at 50% 50%, ${stops.join(', ')})`;
}

// Underline: left-to-right linear sweep
function underlineRevealGradient(progress) {
  const sweepPct = progress * 100, fadePct = FADE_ZONE * 100;
  const solidEnd = Math.max(0, sweepPct - fadePct);
  const stops = [`rgba(0,0,0,1) 0%`, `rgba(0,0,0,1) ${solidEnd.toFixed(1)}%`];
  for (let i = 0; i <= NOISE_STOPS; i++) {
    const t = i / NOISE_STOPS, pct = solidEnd + t * fadePct;
    if (pct > sweepPct) break;
    const baseAlpha = 1 - t;
    const noise = (Math.random() - 0.5) * 2 * NOISE_STRENGTH;
    const alpha = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, baseAlpha + noise * (1 - Math.abs(baseAlpha - 0.5) * 2)));
    stops.push(`rgba(0,0,0,${alpha.toFixed(3)}) ${pct.toFixed(1)}%`);
  }
  stops.push(`rgba(0,0,0,0) ${sweepPct.toFixed(1)}%`, `rgba(0,0,0,0) 100%`);
  return `linear-gradient(to right, ${stops.join(', ')})`;
}

function animateReveal(el, gradientFn, duration) {
  el.style.opacity = '1';
  let start = null;
  function frame(now) {
    if (!start) start = now;
    const raw = Math.min((now - start) / duration, 1);
    const grad = gradientFn(easeInOutCubic(raw));
    el.style.webkitMaskImage = grad;
    el.style.maskImage = grad;
    el.style.webkitMaskSize = '100% 100%';
    el.style.maskSize = '100% 100%';
    if (raw < 1) requestAnimationFrame(frame);
    else { el.style.webkitMaskImage = ''; el.style.maskImage = ''; }
  }
  requestAnimationFrame(frame);
}

// Usage:
// animateReveal(circleEl, circleRevealGradient, 700);
// animateReveal(underlineEl, underlineRevealGradient, 500);

Footer Structure

Standard footer across all social posts. Three elements, always the same. Weight: 95

PlatformFooter PaddingWeight
Instagrampadding: 22px 68px85
LinkedInpadding: 18px 72px85
Copy: Footer HTML + CSS
HTML + CSS 
/* Footer Structure (Weight: 95)
   Left: flag icon + separator + We-Commerce wordmark
   Right: Fluid dots mark
   Subtle, never competing with content
*/

.footer {
  position: absolute;
  bottom: 0;
  left: 0;
  right: 0;
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: center;
  padding: 22px 68px;     /* Instagram. LinkedIn: 18px 72px */
  z-index: 10;
}
.footer-left {
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  gap: 12px;
}
.footer-left img {
  height: 18px;
  opacity: 0.8;
}
.footer-separator {
  width: 1px;
  height: 14px;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.15);
}
.footer-right img {
  height: 22px;
  opacity: 0.8;
}

/* HTML */
<footer class="footer">
  <div class="footer-left">
    <img src="assets/logos/wecommerce-flags.png" alt="We-Commerce">
    <div class="footer-separator"></div>
    <img src="assets/logos/wecommerce-logos.png" alt="We-Commerce">
  </div>
  <div class="footer-right">
    <img src="assets/logos/frame-3-fluid-dots.png" alt="Fluid">
  </div>
</footer>

FLFont Tagline Patterns

The handwritten font for the takeaway line. Confidence and personality. Weight: 90

Size Examples

Every transaction matters.

32px — Instagram primary tagline

One connection. Zero 3am calls.

26px — Instagram secondary tagline

One platform. Every transaction.

22px — LinkedIn tagline

Placement Patterns

When a transaction fails

Every transaction gets its best shot.

Placement: Below headline, before footer. Never IN the headline.

PatternExample
[benefit]. [contrast]."One connection. Zero 3am calls."
[declaration]."Every transaction matters."
[scope]. [claim]."One platform. Every transaction."
Copy: FLFont tagline component
CSS + HTML 
/* FLFont Tagline Rules:
   - Taglines and emphasis ONLY, never body/headlines (Weight: 90)
   - Below headline or bottom of content, before footer (Weight: 80)
   - Instagram: 26-32px, LinkedIn: 20-24px
   - Color: post accent color
   - Pattern: [benefit]. [contrast or reinforcement].
*/

.tagline {
  font-family: 'flfontbold', cursive;
  font-size: 28px;        /* Instagram: 26-32px */
  color: #FF8B58;          /* Replace with post accent color */
  line-height: 1.3;
  margin-top: 16px;
}

/* HTML */
<p class="tagline">Every transaction matters.</p>

Layout Archetypes

6 proven layout types for social posts. Each validated through 10+ iterations.

A. Full-Bleed Headline 85

HEADLINE (82-100px)
FLFont tagline
Body (1-2 sentences max)
Footer
Brushstrokes: 2

Brand statements, problem declarations, emotional hooks

B. Headline + Diagram Card 80

HEADLINE (82px)
Diagram / Terminal / Flow
rgba(255,255,255,0.03) bg
Footer

Technical differentiators, before/after, process explanations

C. Giant Stat Hero 80

82%
Headline below stat
Body context
Footer
Circle: around stat

Proof points, reframes, "the number you're not tracking"

D. Pull Quote / Manifesto 80

"
Quote text (50-52px)
FLFont attribution
Footer

Company voice, emotional storytelling, thought leadership

E. Two-Column (LinkedIn only) 75

55% width
Headline
Body text
45% width
Visual element

Educational content, comparisons. 1200x627 landscape ONLY.

F. Centered Manifesto 75

EVERY TRANSACTION
Centered headline
Centered body
Footer
Watermark: ~4% opacity

Mission statements, campaign anchors. Heavy brush framing both sides.

Copy: Layout archetype reference table
Reference 
/* Layout Selection Guide
   See brand/layout-archetypes.md for full specs

   A. Full-Bleed Headline  — Brand statements, emotional hooks
   B. Headline + Diagram   — Technical differentiators, process
   C. Giant Stat Hero      — Proof points, reframes
   D. Pull Quote           — Thought leadership, storytelling
   E. Two-Column           — LinkedIn ONLY (1200x627), comparisons
   F. Centered Manifesto   — Mission statements, campaign anchors

   General Rules:
   - Headline-dominant, not information-dense (Weight: 90)
   - One idea per post (Weight: 85)
   - No stat cards or data grids on social (Weight: 80)
*/
